cards.title: Data entry, ₹15K/month for 2 hours
cards.script: Promises easy work-from-home job (data entry, form filling, copy-paste). Asks for a registration / training / kit fee before you can start. The 'job' does not exist.
cards.example: Data entry job, 2 hrs/day, ₹15K/month. Send 250 registration fee on WhatsApp.

cards.title: WFH offer asking for Aadhaar / PAN
cards.script: Tells you you've been 'shortlisted' for a work-from-home role and asks you to send a photo of Aadhaar / PAN to verify before the offer letter.
cards.example: Work from home opportunity, send your Aadhaar and PAN to this WhatsApp.

cards.title: 'Training kit' before joining
cards.script: Offer comes with a high salary and 'limited slots'. To start, you must pay a 'one-time training kit' or 'starter kit' fee.
cards.example: Limited slots! Earn ₹50,000/month, pay ₹2000 for training kit today only.

cards.title: 'Direct selection' without interview
cards.script: An offer letter without an interview, signed by an HR you can't verify. Asks for 'GST charges' to release the offer letter.
cards.example: Congratulations! Offer letter ready. Pay ₹800 GST charges to receive the offer letter.

red_flag_rules.red_flag: They are asking for money upfront (registration / training / deposit / GST). Genuine employers in India NEVER ask candidates to pay any fee. This is illegal under the Indian Employment Exchanges Act.
The pay vs the work hours is too good to be true (e.g. ₹15K/month for 2 hours/day). No legitimate Indian employer pays this rate for unskilled part-time work.
'Data entry job + small fee' is one of the most common scam patterns in India. The 'job' does not exist — they take the fee and disappear, or escalate to bigger fees.
Real employers run an interview before sending an offer letter. 'Selected directly' work-from-home offers are almost always fake.
They want you to deal only on WhatsApp with no company website, office address, or HR email. Real employers have a verifiable presence.

next_steps: Do not pay any 'registration', 'training' or 'kit' fee. No genuine job in India requires this.
Ask for the company's website, office address, and an official email (not @gmail.com / @yahoo.com). Search the company on Google + LinkedIn before responding.
If they have already taken money: file a complaint at https://cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.
Report the message as spam on WhatsApp (long-press → Report).
